own_review.point: NPE risk: shell.getData().getClass() is called without a null check; the SWT.Paint display filter fires for composites in ANY shell (tooltips, content-assist popups, progress shells) whose getData() is often null, so the listener can throw NPE and destabilize/crash the test

Guard couples the test to Eclipse LTK INTERNAL class names (org.eclipse.ltk.internal.ui.refactoring.Refactoring*), fragile across platform versions; additionally the 'Refactoring' error dialog may not be an LTK Refactoring* class (could be a JFace MessageDialog/ErrorDialog), in which case the errorDialogOkPressed branch becomes unreachable
Sleep bumped 2000->5000ms is a band-aid that masks flakiness and slows the test; polling for LS readiness via waitForCondition would be more robust
pom.xml download-maven-plugin 1.6.1->1.6.7 bump is unrelated to the test fix described in the PR title (scope creep), though harmless
WIZARD_CLASSNAME_TEMPLATE is misnamed - it is a class-name prefix, not a template; minor naming nit
'static private' modifier order is unconventional; Java convention is 'private static'
getButtons(c) is computed eagerly on every paint event even when the shell title does not match, losing the old early-exit behavior of hasButton; negligible for a test

points.why: Verified: filter fires for all shells; null data shells (tooltips/popups) would NPE inside the display filter

points.point: WIZARD_CLASSNAME_TEMPLATE references internal LTK package, fragile across Eclipse versions
points.why: Verified: org.eclipse.ltk.internal.* is non-API and version-fragile

points.point: Stricter shell filter may skip legitimate dialogs whose getData() class differs
points.why: Real risk: the 'Refactoring' error dialog may not be an LTK Refactoring* class, making that branch unreachable

points.point: Consolidate pressOk/pressCancel duplicated reflection into one helper
points.why: Verified duplication; concrete actionable DRY cleanup

points.point: pom plugin bump reasonable, confirm changelog
points.why: Restates the change with generic advice; misses the scope-creep angle

missed: Sleep increase 2000->5000ms is a band-aid; should poll for LS readiness instead
Human point: MANIFEST.MF version bump question (file not in provided diff; counted uniformly)

points.point: NPE if shell.getData() returns null, transient shells may lack data
points.why: Verified real; correct reasoning about transient shells firing Paint events

points.point: Increased sleep masks underlying issue; use wait-for-condition pattern
points.why: Verified: hardcoded 5s sleep remains fragile on CI; actionable alternative given

points.point: getButtons allocates a HashSet on every call
points.why: Self-admittedly negligible in a test; suggested 'fix' is muddled

points.point: Mnemonic '&' in BUTTON_CONTINUE/BUTTON_BACK constants could break if Eclipse changes mnemonic handling
points.why: Pre-existing string literals merely moved to constants; speculative FYI, no real action

missed: Fragile dependency on internal LTK class-name prefix (org.eclipse.ltk.internal.*) and risk the new filter excludes legitimate dialogs
Human point: MANIFEST.MF version bump question (file not in provided diff; counted uniformly)

points.point: NPE when shell.getData() is null; Paint filter fires on ALL composites, many shells have no data
points.why: Verified real; most precise explanation of why null data occurs

points.point: Internal LTK class-name dependency fragile; keep shell-text check primary, class check secondary with null safety
points.why: Verified non-API dependency; concrete remediation proposed

points.point: Sleep 2000->5000 makes test 150% slower; use readiness check instead
points.why: Verified band-aid; actionable (though a comment about LS init already exists, it does not justify the increase)
True but negligible for a test; v4 itself says keep as-is for readability

points.point: Making private helpers static adds no value and reduces consistency
points.why: Pure style opinion; static private helpers are harmless (the real nit is 'static private' modifier order, which v4 did not make)

points.point: pom plugin bump unrelated to test fix, should be separate PR
points.why: Verified scope creep: pluginManagement bump in root pom is unrelated to the test fix

missed: Human point: MANIFEST.MF version bump question (file not in provided diff; counted uniformly)

qualitative: All three candidates caught the single most important defect — the unguarded shell.getData() NPE inside a global SWT.Paint filter — which neither the human reference nor a careless reader would flag. v4 is the best overall: it uniquely covered all three substantive axes (NPE with the most accurate mechanism explanation, internal-LTK-API fragility with a concrete primary/secondary remediation, and the unrelated pom.xml scope creep), missing only the human's MANIFEST.MF point which was not present in the provided diff. v2 is a close second with the strongest treatment of the dialog-filter restrictiveness risk and a legitimate DRY suggestion for pressOk/pressCancel, but it missed the sleep band-aid entirely. v3 is the weakest: it missed the internal-API fragility — the core design risk of the PR — and padded its review with speculative filler (mnemonic characters, HashSet allocation). No candidate fabricated anything; the characteristic weakness across all three is filler points about negligible test-code efficiency and benign version bumps. The human reference point targets a MANIFEST.MF change absent from the diff given to candidates, so it was counted as missed uniformly and does not affect ranking.
